assessment_items.question_content:
The subject of this tympanum is associated with which series of events in the Late Middle Ages?

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f2ba1cccfcb8330d10264d31285846e7.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This scene represents Pentecost or the Mission to the Apostles, where Christ directed his closest followers to preach his message in other places.

assessment_items.question_content:
Images of heaven and hell caused medieval people to consider their eternal fate.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Viewing images of the Last Judgment caused medieval people to consider their eternal fate.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figures of Adam and Eve do not seem to care about the consequences of eating the fruit.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/996ad5f84c8bca88b85f3e95ee52f5b3.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Romanesque art begins to show the emotive responses of human figures.

assessment_items.question_content:
This painting of the Wise and Foolish Virgins reflects the influence of which artistic tradition?

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/009b44e0ea296591cedb105c70bca186.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: At this time, Constantinople was the major art center in Christendom. Artists in other places looked to Constantinople for inspiration.

choices.content: Hiberno-Saxon Art of Ireland and Northern England

assessment_items.question_content:
Relics were held to have spiritual power—to effect healing miracles and time off from Purgatory.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Relics were powerful objects associated with saints.

assessment_items.question_content:
The semicircular space above a Romanesque portal is called a . . . 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c6f742f7954134866e8a8ae790787aed.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This portal is from St. Lazare and the tynpanum depicts the Last Judgment.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Second and Third Crusades were announced at . . .

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Vézelay, in France, is on one of the major pilgrimage routes through France.
